programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern try to help the Miller family in North Lincolnshire, with parents Wes and Mel wanting to save up for their wedding after 10 years together. With no savings and big expectations, they need to make considerable changes if they want the wedding of their dreams - can Alex and Steph show these big spenders that it is possible to create memories without breaking the bank, and help them put money aside for their special day? The Great British public gets their say too, as they put four similar-looking biker jackets to the test(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A return visit to the historic Abbey Pumping station in Leicester, now the city's Museum of Technology, which complete with four working beam engines is a fine example of Victorian engineering. Expert Ronnie Archer-Morgan swoons over some 1960s steel sculptures, while books specialist Rupert Powell meets playwright Joe Orton's sister who brings in the typewriter on which he wrote some of his most important scripts. Plus, Amin Jaffer discovers a valuable collection of exotic metal boxes - they look like snuff boxes but their actual purpose is much more intriguing. Presented by Fiona Bruce(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: The vet is called when Violet the koala goes downhill, while Iggy Pop the iguana has a mystery tail injury that requires surgery - but at his age an operation is a risky business(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don and the team focus their attention on the loss of wildflower habitats in the UK and the part that gardeners can play in averting the resulting loss of wildlife. In Lincolnshire, Adam Frost finds out how crucial meadows are to supporting the ecosystem, Arit Anderson visits East Sussex to discover the results of a recent wildlife survey carried out in the gardens there and Nick Bailey travels to Staffordshire where meadows are being grown in gardens for the benefit of wildlife. Plus, Carol Klein identifies plants that can help increase insect and bird populations(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Lindsey Chapman and Martin Hughes-Games join team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke on the panel show that tests wildlife knowledge, with Kate Humble asking the questions(n)

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: In Heaton Mersey, Manchester, Zoe wants her porch revamped and a new front door. She has £1,200 to spend and asks three pairs of tradesmen to put in a pitch(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott and Len Goodman visit east London, where the former Strictly Come Dancing judge returns to his childhood sampling some Jewish delicacies and has his first taste of Vietnamese food. Chef Ainsley heads to the kitchen and cooks a mouth-watering dish inspired by the day's adventures(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate Britain's love of seafood and the plentiful and diverse supply of produce obtained from local waters. They also explore the history of Billingsgate Market in east London, the country's largest inland fish market(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Megan helps to rehome 80 caged hens with local families in Fife, and when one is discovered with a broken leg, she decides to take care of it. In Teesdale, farm vet Richard takes drastic action to save the life of a calf with bloat, while Helen meets a Jack Russell puppy that has bitten its own tongue. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Life Story
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how the competition to breed creates scenes of the most extraordinary beauty, as well as some of the most violent battles seen in nature. A male flame bowerbird shows off its artistic prowess to impress a potential mate - but if that does not prove enough, it will demonstrate its dancing skills instead. Waved albatrosses pair up for life and spend hours in each other's company, although things are not so simple for a male peacock jumping spider - one wrong move in its dazzling courtship routine could prove fatal. However, one of the most magnificent displays of all is created by a tiny male pufferfish, which impresses the ladies by building a spectacular underwater 'crop circle' in the sand - arguably the most perfect and complex structure created by any animal(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: James Braxton and Roo Irvine head back up the north-west coast on the hunt for precious artefacts. En route Roo heads far out into Morecambe Bay to look for a local delicacy and James runs away to Blackpool to join the circus. Roo finds an object with equine interest, but will it make enough cash at the York auction for her to pip James to the greater profit(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Sophie Faldo, David Domoney, Frances Tophill, James Tanner and Will Kirk form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, with consumers unboxing, trying out and giving their feedback on each invention(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: University Challenge icons Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull take a road trip with a difference, searching Scotland, England, Northern Ireland and Wales for hidden gems of British scientific and technological ingenuity. They begin with a trip to Blackpool Pleasure Beach to experience Sir Hiram Maxim's 1904 ‘Captive Flying Machines', followed by a visit to Southport, home to the only lawnmower museum of its kind in the world(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys presents the first heat of the latest series of the classic quiz, where contenders sit upon the famous black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock in an attempt to ultimately become the show's latest champion. The specialist subjects in this opening edition are the Lake District, the novels of Sue Townsend, horror writer Stephen King and celebrated chemist Dorothy Hodgkin(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer brave the wild lakes of Essex in search of the mighty carp. They have the latest high-tech equipment to catch the biggest fish they can - and are fully prepared to stay all night if they have to. The pair discuss the true meaning of a mid-life crisis and decide to visit a beauty clinic to see whether there are any suitable treatments available to re-capture their youth. Bob reveals his secret recipe to make the perfect scrambled eggs - but it's not the only thing that shocks Paul(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty advises on essential summer pruning and adds French beans to the vegetable garden. The programme also meets a gardener in Hertfordshire who combines her love of cottage garden plants with tropical plants and visits a garden in Kent where pots are the stars of the summer show(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: The early 1970s finds Bob focused on editing the musical movie Cabaret. Gwen, meanwhile, auditions for the leading role in a straight play that forces her to reflect on the beginning of her career, her disastrous first marriage to James Henaghan and a career change that led to her breakthrough performance in Can-Can. Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams star in this biographical drama detailing the lives of choreographer-director Bob Fosse and celebrated Broadway dancer Gwen Verdon. With Norbert Leo Butz and Peter Scolari(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Joe Lycett, Phil Wang and Ellie Taylor join regular panellist Alan Davies, as questionmaster Sandi Toksvig looks at the pastimes of past times. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the panellists attempt to answer the fiendishly difficult questions, with points invariably awarded for the answers the questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Sarah Smith(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Edinburgh Nights with Nish Kumar
programme.desc: Nish goes behind the scenes with some of the big names and hopeful first timers in comedy at the largest arts festival in the world. He samples the Los Angeles Philharmonic Orchestra playing for free in a football stadium, marvels at trail biking legend Danny MacAskill creating an extraordinary circus show and tries his hand at beatboxing(n)

programme.title: A Most Violent Year
programme.desc: New York City during the winter of 1981 is a dangerous place to be, but one immigrant is determined to expand his business and support his family. Unfortunately, the unrelenting rise in violent crime threatens to ruin his livelihood and destroy everything he has worked tirelessly for. Thriller, starring Oscar Isaac, Jessica Chastain and David Oyelowo(n)

credits.actor: Oscar Isaac, Jessica Chastain, David Oyelowo

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Stacey Meets the IS Brides - Panorama
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley travels to holding camps in Kurdish-controlled northern Syria, where she meets Western women who left their home countries to join Islamic State, and also finds the camps filled with European children. The Kurds in control of this part of Syria say the Western powers should not expect them to be jailers on their behalf indefinitely, and Stacey explores the question of what to do with these women and children who have been left behind(n)

programme.title: Extinction Rebellion: Last Chance to Save the World?
programme.desc: Reporter Ben Zand follows climate-change protest group Extinction Rebellion for four months, as they build toward 'The Rebellion' - 11 days of protest in April - with the aim of taking over and occupying various locations in London. The film also focuses on three young people who have been inspired to join and lead the protests, and Ben speaks to the leader of the movement Roger Hallam, who has spent years academically researching tactics for social change. Ben challenges him on his methods and asks whether it is justifiable to encourage young people to break the law(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Featuring Norfolk's World Championship Thumb Wrestling(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Radio host Mark Wright and CBBC vlogger Lauren Layfield enlist expert designers and builders to construct dream dens for lucky youngsters(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: The team has made it to the final but it feels like nobody has faith in them(n)

programme.title: Deadly Top 10
programme.desc: Steve Backshall chooses his favourite creatures that live in extreme environments, including the snow leopard and a deep-diving whale(n)

programme.title: Penguins on a Plane: Great Animal Moves
programme.desc: Despite the ratings-grabbing title, this two-parter isn't quite the action thriller it sounds. Rather, it's an insightful documentary revealing how thousands of exotic animals are moved around the world every day, and how they cope with being transported. First up, a flock of gentoo penguins get a luxury £40,000 container for their trip from New Zealand to Birmingham, while staff at the West Midlands Safari Park prepare to bid farewell to Pinky the two-ton hippo, who puts her trip to the south of France in danger by testing a reinforced crate to its limits. Plus, a beekeeper awaits a precious cargo of some eight million bees, which he hopes will help British fruit farmers(n)

programme.title: Cats v Dogs: Which Is Best?
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Liz Bonnin present the second of two programmes in which pooches and pussies are put through a number of tests to see which family pet comes out on top. There are five more rounds, concentrating on which animal is easiest to train, communication, which is best at helping reduce people's stress, independence and the big question - do cats and dogs really love their owners? On the search for answers, dog-lover Chris meets a Border Collie which can recognise more than 150 words and Liz meets a cat capable of choosing its own TV channels(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Lynn and Sandra in the Highlands get a surprise when they harvest their first batch of honey. The Rodgers family gather to give bull Mario a good send-off, while Raymond and Johnny discover their sheep are under threat from flystrike, and the only way to protect them is to put the entire flock of 1,600 through the sheepdip(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Bryony Crutcher, 26, admits to passing off shop-bought food as her own creations, but now she's been asked to host her best friend's hen party and needs Lorraine Pascale's help to come up with an impressive meal. The food writer puts together an inspiring canape menu for Bryony to master, including Thai larb gai, bite-sized chocolate Napoleons and delicate coconut meringues filled with ginger cream(n)

programme.title: Secret Britain
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Adam Henson explore the moors of North Yorkshire. Adam meets a farmer whose father uncovered a Roman villa while ploughing, attempts to make a clay pot using 2,000-year-old technology and discovers the remains of a forgotten glass-making industry. Ellie finds out why Britain's highest pub, the Tan Hill Inn, was built miles away from the nearest customers, investigates the Whitby Yards - former slums that have become highly sought as havens of peace - and visits the ruins of Rievaulx Abbey(n)

programme.title: Animals at Play
programme.desc: Part two of two. The secrets behind animals' group games, from dolphins that play catch to a hyena tug of war, revealing the benefits they gain from their playful behaviour. The programme examines how breaching the rules of a game can have fatal consequences - but how it also presents an opportunity for animals with a rigid hierarchy to break down social barriers(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the second and penultimate day of the biennial event, held this year at Zdzislaw Krzyszkowiak Stadium in Bydgoszcz, Poland. Events taking place today include the men's and women's 100m and 400m finals, as well as the men's 1500m and women's 3,000m, with Great Britain and holders Germany among the 12 teams involved(n)

programme.title: Proms Encore
programme.desc: Katie Derham is joined by composer Hannah Kendall and trombonist Peter Moore as Proms Encore celebrates the next generation of classical musicians. Rachel Parris discovers what makes the new stars tick, Tom Service takes a musical trip with a maestro and soprano Fatma Said gives a special performance on the bandstand(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.sub-title: The Two and a Half Feathers
programme.desc: Jones's glorious military reputation is called into question when new platoon member George Clarke makes scandalous allegations about his conduct at the Battle of Omdurman in Sudan. Comedy first aired in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe, Clive Dunn and John Le Mesurier(n)

credits.actor: Arthur Lowe, Clive Dunn, John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part two of two. Experts analyse the Princess's marriage to Lord Snowdon at the start of the 1960s. At home with celebrities and artists, they're riding the wave of a cultural and sexual revolution that's transforming Britain. The mystique of monarchy is being increasingly undermined by the emergence of a more open and egalitarian society(n)

programme.title: Don't Look Back
programme.desc: DA Pennebaker's superb, intimate film diary of Bob Dylan's 1965 tour of Britain, chronicling reactions as his tunes caught the nation off-guard, but ultimately won him critical acclaim and worldwide fame and fortune. Featuring interviews with various Sixties cult figures, including the man himself, his then-girlfriend Joan Baez and poet Allen Ginsberg(n)

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Paper Boi is quick to regret his decision to play a show at a college campus that Earn arranged. Comedy drama about a rapper and his manager(n)

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: A flashback to Earn and Al's adolescence, where the authenticity of Earn's shirt is embarrassingly called into question at school(n)

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Earn runs around town trying to finish everything before catching a plane so that his rapper cousin Alfred, aka Paper Boi, can go on a European tour with Clark County. Comedy drama, starring Donald Glover, about an aspiring music manager trying to turn his life around by making his rapper cousin a star(n)

credits.actor: Comedy drama, starring Donald Glover, about an aspiring music manager trying to turn his life around by making his rapper cousin a

programme.title: Clouds of Sils Maria
programme.desc: A famous actress prepares to star in a new production of the play that made her career in a supporting part, with the lead role now played by a scandalous Hollywood star. She and her assistant head to a cottage in the Swiss mountains to prepare, which leads her to reflect on the harsh truths about how her life has changed. Drama, starring Juliette Binoche, Kristen Stewart and Chloe Grace Moretz(n)

credits.actor: Juliette Binoche, Kristen Stewart, Chloe Grace Moretz

programme.title: Stations of the Cross
programme.desc: A girl from a strict Catholic family is an outsider in her secular school, and faces constant reproach from her fundamentalist mother. Under the tuition of a misguided priest, she pushes herself to extreme acts of self-sacrifice in her pursuit of spiritual redemption. Drama, starring Lea van Acken and Florian Stetter. In German, French and Latin(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden explores Hampshire by hot-air balloon, beginning at Mottisfont Abbey in Romsey, where she meets a gardening veteran who is preparing to hand over his duties to a young protegee. She visits the Sir Harold Hillier Gardens, and also climbs into a pair of waders for a go at an unusual kind of weeding(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty advises on essential summer pruning and adds French beans to the vegetable garden. The programme also meets a gardener in Hertfordshire who combines her love of cottage garden plants with tropical plants and visits a garden in Kent where pots are the stars of the summer show(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team heads to Norfolk, where John Craven meets the team behind a corncrake breeding programme, while Margherita Taylor meets artist Shauna Richardson and a sheepdog trainer who also breeds red squirrels. Tom Heap looks at how devices designed to scare seals away from salmon stocks could be having unintended consequences and Adam Henson is in Kent to meets a young shepherdess making a name for herself in the sheepdog handling world(n)

programme.title: The Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Kirsty shows how to make a miniature patio pond, Janie Gall demonstrates an easy cut flower arrangement and George visits an award-winning garden in Livingston, West Lothian(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Nisha Katona takes Fred Sirieix to San Sebastian for a mind-blowing meal at one of the world's top restaurants and to visit a tiny sports bar to learn the secrets of the perfect tortilla. The first place in Nisha's little black book is Mugaritz, ranked as the ninth best restaurant in the world and famous for wild experimentation - something that is made clear as soon as the menus arrive. Instead of a list of dishes to select, the Mugaritz menu is two pictures, one of a swimming fish, one of a flying fish, and Nisha and Fred make their choice, but have little clue what they are going to eat(n)

programme.title: Top of the Shop with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: In the second heat, four food producers with fledgling businesses come to test out their products on the locals in Malhamdale, in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales. This episode sees cheese makers from across the country - Gillian, Jo, Pedro and Julie - compete against each other for a place in the final(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work at Giraffe Manor in Kenya, a unique hotel where giraffe, staff and guests co-exist in a 1930s Scottish-style hunting lodge on the edge of Nairobi National Park. The pair travel to the remote but luxurious outpost of Sasaab Lodge, a six-acre plot set within 82,000 acres of land owned by Samburu tribespeople, who receive a fee for every guest that stays and make up 75 per cent of the staff(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the third and final day of the biennial event, held this year at Zdzislaw Krzyszkowiak Stadium in Bydgoszcz, Poland. Events taking place today include men's 110m hurdles and 800m and women's 1,500m and 5,000m, before proceedings are brought to a close in the traditional manner with the men's and women's 4x100m and 4x400m relays(n)

programme.title: International Rugby Union
programme.desc: England v Wales. John Inverdale presents action from both teams' first World Cup warm-up match, held at Twickenham. Wales prevailed 21-13 when the sides met at Principality Stadium in this year's Six Nations and went on to win the Grand Slam. With analysis from Jonathan Davies and Jeremy Guscott(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a clothing factory in South Shields that manufactures 650 waxed jackets a day. Cherry Healey investigates the science behind waterproof clothing, discovering how a layer of material can keep rain in while allowing sweat to escape, as well as discovering 150-year-old methods of making umbrellas. Plus, Ruth Goodman visits a Scottish harbour to discover the origins of weatherproof coats(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, founder of Crafter's Companion, is a new addition to the panel on the business ideas contest, joining returning dragons Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani in putting more would-be entrepreneurs' ideas to the test. Making their pitches this week are the creator of a brand of spiced rum, who gives the studio an exciting new look, a couple who have brought their four-legged friend with them to demonstrate a range of pet food, and the inventor of a special toilet gadget. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan visits Bosnia and Herzegovina to discover how this small corner of Eastern Europe has changed since the war, with the aid of a local museum curator. He tries out Sarajevo's abandoned bobsleigh track, a remnant of the 1984 winter Olympics, stays in a 'war hostel', and meets a man who claims to have discovered the world's largest pyramid(n)

programme.title: The Mind of Herbert Clunkerdunk
programme.desc: Herbert gets an important job recording a voiceover, but Bobby is less than impressed by the discovery that he has spent all of their savings on glow-in-the-dark paint. The couple is also called in for an urgent parent-teacher meeting at their son's school, which leads Herbert to reminisce about some unpleasant childhood memories(n)

programme.title: The Mind of Herbert Clunkerdunk
programme.desc: Herbert poses for some flashy new headshots to go in his portfolio, in the hope than one of them will land him an acting job, and he can begin paying for all the things he has been buying on the internet - and hopefully leave enough to pay the council tax bill. However, a bicycle ride and an overlong bath have unforeseen consequences(n)

programme.title: Louis Theroux's Altered States
programme.sub-title: Louis Theroux's Altered States: Take My Baby
programme.desc: The documentary-maker explores how adoption is a big business in California, meeting agencies, facilitators and lawyers matching babies to parents, with the adoption process often costing as much as $50,000. He meets families that have spent vast sums on children they are unable to conceive themselves, and women preparing to give up their babies, hearing the stories of poverty, addiction and abuse that have inspired their decision. He also discovers how the adoption process is open to abuse, and reflects on what happens when a mother has a post-birth change of heart(n)

programme.title: Killed By My Debt
programme.desc: Factual drama telling the story of Jerome Rogers and the destructive power of debt. In January 2015, the south London 19-year-old finally got what he'd been working for - a new motorbike and his first real job as a courier. However, in the hands of bailiffs, two £65 traffic fines rose to over a thousand pounds. Some weeks, take-home pay in his zero-hours job was as low as £12. Under the pressure of his debt, Jerome went to some woods and committed suicide. With Chance Perdomo(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Adrian John introduce the edition first aired on May 5, featuring performances by Joyce Sims, New Order, Magnum, Harry Enfield, the Adventures, Narada, Prince, the Christians, the Primitives, S'Express and Star Turn on 45 Pints(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: John Wilson's virtuoso orchestra celebrates the music of one of the most famous film studios, Warner Brothers. The lush sounds of composers such as Max Steiner and Erich Korngold are among the many pieces played showcasing why this historic studio holds a special place in the hearts of lovers of film music. Katie Derham presents from the Royal Albert Hall(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.sub-title: BBC Proms: The Sound of Space
programme.desc: At the Royal Albert Hall, Cerys Matthews introduces a Prom featuring music from sci-fi films. The London Contemporary Orchestra performs excerpts from cult soundtracks together with recent works from Hans Zimmer and Mica Levi. Fans of Alien: Covenant, Interstellar, Under the Skin, or Gravity will recognise this repertoire(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Adrian John introduce the edition first aired on May 5, featuring performances by Joyce Sims, New Order, Magnum, Harry Enfield, the Adventures, Narada, Prince, the Christians, the Primitives, S'Express and Star Turn on 45 Pints(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll Guns for Hire: The Story of the Sideman
programme.desc: Earl Slick, David Bowie's sideman of 40 years, looks at the men and women behind some of the greatest songs of the 20th and 21st century. The sidemen are often the forgotten 'guns for hire' that changed musical history and this documentary reveals what it feels like to live in the shadow of a star, a story of triumph and thwarted ambition. Contributors include Mick Jagger, Keith Richards and Billy Joel, as well as sidemen past and present for Prince, David Bowie, John Lennon, Otis Redding, the Rolling Stones and Beyonce(n)

programme.title: Reggae Fever: David Rodigan
programme.desc: The life of the German-born reggae broadcaster and radio presenter whose love affair with Jamaican music began in the 1950s, celebrating a man whose career is strangely intertwined with not only the evolution of such music in this country, but also the evolution of the culture. In recent years, the 67-year-old's DJ appearances have started attracting a far younger audience, a reflection perhaps of the way different forms of music from the different cultures that have arrived in Britain over the last 70 years have integrated, taken root and spawned new scenes, attitudes and tastes(n)

programme.title: The Last Pirates: Britain's Rebel DJs
programme.desc: Rodney P's career as a rapper would not have been possible without the lifeblood of pirate radio airplay in the 1980s. Here, he presents an alternative history of Britain during a time of entrepreneurialism and social upheaval, with archive and music that celebrates a very different side of Thatcher's Britain. His story details how land-based pirate radio broadcasters created a platform for black music, changing the soundtrack of Britain for ever. Featuring contributions by DJs, station owners and Department of Trade and Industry enforcers(n)

programme.title: Lost Land of the Volcano
programme.desc: Scientists and wildlife film-makers led by Steve Backshall, George McGavin and Gordon Buchanan explore Mount Bosavi, a giant extinct volcano covered by thick and largely unexplored rainforest in New Guinea. With the help of trackers from a remote tribe, they search for rare creatures and species to find evidence that may help stop the area's trees being felled for timber. The nest of the world's smallest parrot, an unusual beetle and new types of frog, gecko and bat are among the team's first discoveries(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates the story of some of New Zealand's wildest places, home to hyper-intelligent parrots, snails with teeth, and constellations of glow-worms. In these often inhospitable locations, any creature hoping to survive must resort to radical techniques, none more so than on the South Island, which lies toward Antarctica and is home to the rapidly rising peaks of the Southern Alps(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: June's kidnappers threaten to kill the ferry's passengers unless she gives herself up, while the authorities, alerted by a silent alarm, prepare a plan of action. In Danish(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: Yusuf warns Danish intelligence that if any attempt is made to rescue the ferry hostages they will all be killed. Back on shore, SP and Simon are visited by Mahdi's wife. In Danish(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.desc: Ten years in the making, this series tells the epic story of the Vietnam War, bringing the war and the chaotic epoch it encompassed viscerally to life. In this opening part, Vietnamese revolutionaries led by Ho Chi Minh end nearly a century of French colonial occupation and, with the Cold War intensifying, Vietnam is divided in two at Geneva(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.desc: President Kennedy and his advisers wrestle with how deeply to get involved in South Vietnam, as the increasingly autocratic Diem regime faces a growing communist insurgency(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell introduce the edition from April 24, featuring Pat and Mick, James Brown, Fairground Attraction, Brenda Russell, the Primitives, Luther Vandross, Joyce Sims, Bananarama, Will Downing, S'Express and Scott Fitzgerald(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: Love and loss, life and death collide in a version of Mozart's Requiem by Nathalie Stutzmann and the BBC National Orchestra and Chorus of Wales, featuring soloists Fatma Said, Kathryn Rudge, Sunnyboy Dladla and David Shipley, from the Royal Albert Hall(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Horizon: Tim Peake Special - How to Be an Astronaut
programme.desc: Highlights of a video diary filmed over two years by Tim Peake as he prepared to become the first British astronaut on board the International Space Station(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.sub-title: The Sky at Night Guides: Galaxies
programme.desc: Chris Lintott uncovers the deepest secrets of the galaxies. Using the Sky at Night archives he explores the first galaxy ever discovered, today's cutting-edge methods to map the Milky Way and goes beyond to the edge of the universe(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The programme goes behind the scenes as the European Space Agency selects its next mission, which will be launched in 2028, meeting British teams vying to have their ideas selected, including a revolutionary mission to a comet(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines the social significance of cars in the 20th century. He begins by travelling to Germany, Italy and Russia to examine how dictators kick-started the mobilization of the masses, finding tales of design brilliance, abject failure, war, fraud and double dealing. James also learns about how the British motor industry blew a gift-wrapped chance to rule the world and he gets his own back with a stunt that means bad news for one of the planet's most hated cars(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary about the final joint assignment of war correspondent Marie Colvin and photographer Paul Conroy to cover the siege of Homs in Syria in 2012, during which Colvin was killed by artillery fire. The film tells the story of the pair's fateful mission and Conroy's epic battle to escape the city to tell the world of his fallen colleague and the plight of the people of Homs(n)

Self-appointed leader and lifestyle guru, Jurgen, offers some sage advice to the tiny tiger cub, until he too gets spooked by the prospect of facing such a big predator. Just at that moment, the Furball disappears, only for them to spot him up on the balcony, they're soon chasing him all over the hotel. programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: After the disastrous Open Day at his house, Phil is nowhere to be found. As Edith and Sandra hurry to Phil's house, Edith receives a phone call from her 'difficult' sister Joan inviting herself to stay. Sandra pulls faces, but Edith feels obliged to agree. The duo enter Phil's house where they find a piano-playing Queenie, but no Phil. Roger visits the recovering Bob in hospital and tells him that he has thought of a new way to make money and he has let his mother's spare room, online, to two Americans. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

credits.actor: Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese, Sue Johnston

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and her team rescue and revamp a green enamel light shade, a 1970s sideboard and a 1930s Lloyd Loom chair in the hope of making a profit(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern try to help the Miller family in North Lincolnshire, with parents Wes and Mel wanting to save up for their wedding after 10 years together. With no savings and big expectations, they need to make considerable changes if they want the wedding of their dreams - can Alex and Steph show these big spenders that it is possible to create memories without breaking the bank, and help them put money aside for their special day? The Great British public gets their say too, as they put four similar-looking biker jackets to the test(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway finds hidden treasures at opposite ends of Wales and discovers his artistic side. Both walks show the amazing places tucked just a stone's throw from busy Welsh towns. Singer Mike Peters from the Alarm is Derek's guide in the hills above Prestatyn - guitar in hand. In Newport, Derek finds that a smart phone or a tablet can take the place of easel and paints - but how will his picture turn out? A beautiful walk along a canal with 14 locks finds the weatherman sheltering from a monsoon under the M4(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Samantha and Shauna celebrate the achievements of Clwyd Special Riding School in north Wales with a big cook-out. With more than 100 people to feed, Sam and Shauna organise a menu of 10 huge beef briskets, barbecued in giant cinder-block smoker, built with the help of the community(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team is in Perthshire, where Matt Baker visits Cultybraggan, a former PoW camp that is now a thriving community of artisans, craftspeople and food producers. Charlotte Smith jumps in a kayak and takes to the water on a wildlife safari, while Steve Borwn meets women growing tea in the south Highlands and learns the art of making the perfect cuppa(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A return visit to the historic Abbey Pumping station in Leicester, now the city's Museum of Technology, which complete with four working beam engines is a fine example of Victorian engineering. Expert Ronnie Archer-Morgan swoons over some 1960s steel sculptures, while books specialist Rupert Powell meets playwright Joe Orton's sister who brings in the typewriter on which he wrote some of his most important scripts. Plus, Amin Jaffer discovers a valuable collection of exotic metal boxes - they look like snuff boxes but their actual purpose is much more intriguing. Presented by Fiona Bruce(n)

programme.title: Poldark
programme.desc: When the banks start issuing paper notes instead of gold, Demelza finds herself thrown into uncharted waters with her workers and the locals, a situation taken advantage of by Tess and Jacka. Morwenna tackles the grief of being parted from her son, while Geoffrey Charles makes plain his intent to marry Cecily, landing both of them in trouble. George conspires once more against Ned and the Poldarks as he and his allies enact a plan to destroy their opposition for good. Drama, starring Aidan Turner(n)

credits.actor: Aidan Turner

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers with a pressing deadline of their own race to refurbish a terraced house in Tylorstown in south Wales, a businesswoman buys a former takeaway to help her business, and a developer got more than he bargains for when he buys a piece of land on the Isle of Sheppey. programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation(n)

programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy in discussion with Labour MP Rachel Reeves about her book Women of Westminster: The MPs Who Changed Politics. First shown on BBC Parliament on Saturday 9 March(n)

programme.title: Briefings

programme.desc: Prime minister Theresa May's speech in Stirling on the future of the Union and the review into the UK government and devolution, from Thursday 4 July(n)

programme.title: Speaker's House
programme.sub-title: Sir Edward Leigh on Michael Heseltine
programme.desc: Sir Edward Leigh delivers a lecture on 'What if Michael Heseltine not John Major had become PM in 1990?' in the Speaker's House from Monday 20 May(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Justice of the Supreme Court Jonathan Sumption explores how democratic processes have the power to accommodate opposition opinions and interests, but argues that in recent years politics has shied away from legislating and now the courts have taken on more and more of the role of making law(n)

programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the Welsh Assembly as First Minister Mark Drakeford tackles queries on issues affecting life in Wales(n)

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: The speech by the Cabinet Secretary, Sir Mark Sedwill, on future public service challenges at the Institute of Government, from Thursday 13 June.(n)

programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy talks to Tim Bouverie about his new book 'Appeasing Hitler: Chamberlain, Churchill and the Road to War'. First shown on BBC Parliament on Saturday 13 April(n)

programme.title: Briefings
programme.sub-title: Briefings - Gordon Brown on the Union
programme.desc: Former Labour prime minister Gordon Brown making a speech in London on the future of the United Kingdom, from Tuesday 25 June(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption argues that judges - especially those of the European Court of Human Rights - have usurped power by expanding the interpretation of human rights law. He explains that concepts of human rights have a long history in the common law. But by contrast, the European Convention on Human Rights has become a dynamic treaty, taking on new interpretations and powers(n)

programme.title: Mayor's Question Time

programme.title: Briefings

programme.title: Speaker's House
programme.desc: Lord Norton delivers a lecture on 'What if Ted Heath had resigned in October 1974 and Willie Whitelaw had become Tory Leader?' in the Speaker's House from Monday 18 March(n)

programme.title: Briefings
programme.sub-title: Briefings - George Osborne and Niall Ferguson
programme.desc: Former Chancellor and editor of the Evening Standard, George Osborne, in conversation with historian Niall Ferguson at the Margaret Thatcher conference, organised by the Centre for Policy Studies. First shown on BBC Parliament on Saturday 29 June(n)

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Youth Parliament committee on knife crime, focusing on intervention, with evidence from the West Midlands police, the Surrey Police and Crime Commissioner, the Scottish Violence Reduction Unit and Edward Timpson, author of the Timpson review of school exclusion, from Friday 5 July(n)

programme.title: Briefings

programme.title: Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Select Committees

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. The US Constitution is by contrast essentially a legal document. This has led Americans to address what should be political questions via the courts, rather than through politics. Recorded in front of an audience at George Washington University in Washington DC(n)

programme.title: Speaker's House
programme.desc: Professor Dame Nancy Rothwell, Vice Chancellor of the University of Manchester and co-chair of the Prime Minister's Council of Science and Technology, delivers a lecture on 'Communicating Science' in Speaker's House, from Wednesday 15 May(n)

programme.title: Slighean Sgoile an t-Saoghail (Most Dangerous Ways to School)
programme.desc: Lake Titicaca, straddling the border between Peru and Bolivia in the Andes Mountains, is one of South America's largest lakes. Living here are the Uro - an ancient people, described at times as the only decedents of the Incas. They are the people of the floating islands, a very small community of about 2,000 with a community consisting of 50 swimming islands. Every day from the furthest corners, the youngest inhabitants paddle their way in their small boats to school(n)

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)

programme.title: Cuimhneachan (Remembrance)
programme.desc: The Gaelic songs and poetry of the First World War and the stories behind them. Featuring specially recorded performances by Gaelic singers Art Cormack, Linda MacLeod, Murdo MacDonald and Robert Robertson, as well as readings from both songs and poems. The first episode looks at what bards such as Neil MacDonald from North Uist and Duncan Johnston from Islay had to say at the beginning of the war, and later on after direct experience of trench warfare on the battlefields of France(n)

programme.title: Talamh Torrach (Scotland's Food and Drink)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo reaches the end of his journey in Santa Maria Di Leuca. He enjoys a tour of Italy's most southerly church, and cooks a saffron and chicken risotto for the priest and some of the nuns who work there. Gino also visits Leuca's famous waterfall, which was built by Mussolini and is only switched on for special occasions, before heading out by boat to see the end of Italy from the water. For his final dish, Gino cooks locally caught swordfish with rosemary sauteed potatoes and tangy gremolata(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo reaches the end of his journey in Santa Maria Di Leuca. He enjoys a tour of Italy's most southerly church, and cooks a saffron and chicken risotto for the priest and some of the nuns who work there. Gino also visits Leuca's famous waterfall, which was built by Mussolini and is only switched on for special occasions, before heading out by boat to see the end of Italy from the water. For his final dish, Gino cooks locally caught swordfish with rosemary sauteed potatoes and tangy gremolata(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim wants Frank to stop treating her like an invalid, Jack and Sarah have another go at skiing, and Rachel asks Alan for some bar work, but Lynn is not happy about it(n)

programme.desc: Joe is not pleased when Mark tell him he is going out with Lisa, Jack and Sarah's skiing plans are scuppered, and Carol attempts to use her 'healing hands' on Kim(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail is horrified when the police interview her over Carmel's accident, and when Andy starts back at Bettabuy he is impressed by checkout girl Amy Nelson(n)

programme.desc: Martin feels miserable and upset that no one believed him, Andy is pleased when Amy agrees to have lunch with him, and Maggie and Ken plan an Easter holiday in Brittany(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Jewel Robbery at the Grand Metropolitan
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson, Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Robert and his family make their annual summer visit to his Scottish cousins at Duneagle Castle, leaving most of their servants at home. Edith discovers newspaper editor Michael Gregson is also on holiday in the area and may not be there just for the sketching and fishing, while personal servant O'Brien finds a kindred spirit below stairs in the form of her opposite number. Meanwhile, at Downton, Carson faces the task of keeping the staff focused on their work polishing the silverware and cleaning, but with the Granthams hundreds of miles away, minds begin to wander to other things, including the forthcoming Thirsk Country Fair(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica Fletcher tries to clear a friend implicated in the theft of a priceless Egyptian relic - and a murder to boot. Whodunit, starring Angela Lansbury, James Read and Lee Meriwether(n)

credits.actor: Angela Lansbury, James Read, Lee Meriwether

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A case from 13 years earlier returns to haunt Lewis when the evidence comes under review. Graham Lawrie was incarcerated in a secure psychiatric hospital after being found guilty of the murders of three police officers, but now the findings of the forensics lab have been called into question and an appeal has been launched. As Lawrie remains locked up ahead of his day in court, PC Mark Travis is lured to an isolated location with a hoax call and falls victim to a killer who uses the same weapon as that used in the original murders(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As the Second World War continues to rage, Foyle investigates a pro-Hitler organisation he suspects may be linked to a mysterious death at the White Feather Hotel, while reluctant assistant Milner struggles to decide where his loyalties lie. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell, with Charles Dance and Maggie Steed(n)

credits.actor: Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Charles Dance, Maggie Steed

programme.title: Carry On Follow That Camel
programme.desc: A disgraced English gentleman joins the Foreign Legion to restore his honour, and proves himself an unlikely hero. Meanwhile, his naive lost love learns he was innocent, and heads to the desert to bring him home. Comedy, starring Phil Silvers, Jim Dale, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Peter Butterworth, Bernard Bresslaw and Joan Sims(n)

credits.actor: Phil Silvers, Jim Dale, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Peter Butterworth, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims

programme.title: Cilla
programme.desc: Reunited with Bobby, Cilla's confidence returns and she once again dazzles crowds with her vocal talents. The re-emergence of impresario Brian Epstein then propels her career to greater heights, but this is quickly followed by a new low when her first record fails to set the charts alight, despite being written by two local lads called John Lennon and Paul McCartney. Epstein suggests a change of direction, persuading the singer to take a gamble on the ballad Anyone Who Had a Heart, against her future husband's advice. Continuing Jeff Pope's three-part biopic charting Cilla Black's rise to fame in 1960s Liverpool, starring Sheridan Smith, with Aneurin Barnard and Ed Stoppard(n)

credits.actor: Sheridan Smith, Aneurin Barnard, Ed Stoppard

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Vanessa fears for the animals at Mara during a rabies outbreak at the National Park. Danny is asked to bring it under control, but during the quarantine operation he clashes with feisty vet Alice, who has been sent to help him. Evan is thrown a party to celebrate his return to Leopard's Den and Rosie is surprised when elephant calf Tula warms to Charlotte. Starring Stephen Tompkinson(n)

credits.actor: Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Rosie and Max finally get married, but when she is injured by an elephant, a doctor has to give the newlyweds terrible news. Du Plessis is devastated when Caroline decides to stay in England - and Georgina makes enemies when she reveals she encouraged her sister not to return. Fatani declares his love to Vanessa, but realises she has feelings for Danny, while Alice has a risky solution for saving the elephant calf(n)

programme.desc: Stan is attacked by hooligans during a late-night run after the pubs have shut _ so Blakey decides to give the staff a few lessons in self-defence(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist's wife is murdered during an excavation in the Middle East. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Ron Berglas, Georgina Sowerby and Iain Mitchell(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Ron Berglas, Georgina Sowerby, Iain Mitchell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Blue Train
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As the Second World War continues to rage, Foyle investigates a pro-Hitler organisation he suspects may be linked to a mysterious death at the White Feather Hotel, while reluctant assistant Milner struggles to decide where his loyalties lie. credits.actor: Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Charles Dance, Maggie Steed

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Schoolteacher Connie Bishop keeps waking in the night, convinced she has an intruder, but most of the villagers in Badger's Drift dismiss her fears as an overactive imagination. Days later, a local councillor thought to have been obsessed with her is found murdered in her garden, and the ensuing investigation by Barnaby and Jones uncovers jealous rivals, ruthless social climbers and a cottage with a few secrets. Guest starring Paul Kaye, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

credits.actor: Paul Kaye, John Nettles, Jason Hughes

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective investigates the disappearance of a Dutch au pair, which seems connected to rivalry amid the city's academics. His search leads him to a family estate in the Oxfordshire countryside where he meets three siblings divided by personal demons and shared loss. Endeavour suspects there is a link between the disappearance and an unsolved case from years before, but as the investigation continues, conflict erupts between his fellow officers. When a group of teenagers makes a horrifying discovery, he suspects someone is manipulating events from afar(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The rural bliss of Ferne Basset is rudely shattered by the discovery of an elderly resident's drowned body. But when Barnaby and Troy descend on the village to ferret out the culprit, they get caught up in simmering backstage rivalries at a local amateur dramatic production of Amadeus. John Nettles stars, with Daniel Casey, Sarah Badel, Janine Duvitski and Bernard Hepton(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel tries to persuade Mark to take his exam. Carol is late for work again. Eric tries to explain to Michael that he loves Elizabeth(n)

programme.desc: The school tells Joe that Mark did not turn up for his last exam. Seth catches Lorraine at the fish farm trying to steal some trout. Eric has a surprise for Elizabeth(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Weatherill is involved in an air rescue to save an unconscious girl and finds herself braving a cliff abseil to reach the youngster. Dr Ormerod faces a difficult time when he misdiagnoses a patient who is left fighting for life in hospital. Sister Brigid has an accident on her bike, while Harper faces a staff revolt. Medical drama, starring Amy Robbins, Robert Daws, Linda Armstrong and John Axon(n)

credits.actor: Amy Robbins, Robert Daws, Linda Armstrong, John Axon

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An elderly man reports an unusual break-in, during which the uninvited visitor left a mysterious statue on the kitchen table. It soon emerges that it is just the first in a series of unexplained events, as a police phone box arrives and then disappears from his garden, cement is delivered to his house for no apparent reason and a fire is started in his garage. Noakes advises Steve to keep well away as it is not technically a police matter, but the officer decides to investigate further. Rural drama, starring Trevor Ray, Georgie Glen and James Carlton(n)

credits.actor: Trevor Ray, Georgie Glen, James Carlton

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star(n)

credits.actor: Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton, Mark Jordon

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox(n)

credits.actor: Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), David Suchet, Lysette Anthony, Edward Fox

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Hemel Hempstead, Hertfordshire, where they meet deaf couple Asif Iqbal and wife Sabina. The green-fingered experts attempt to turn a boring lawn and crumbling patio into a Bollywood dream garden with an outdoor kitchen for Asif, Sabina and their family(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Nurse Maggie Simkiss cares for end of life patients and treats their emotional well-being as well as their direct medical needs. To say thank you for her hard work, Alan Titchmarsh and his team of green-fingered experts arrive at her Hull home and help transform the unkempt garden into a grown-up Mediterranean hideaway(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Lesley Manville, Alex Jennings

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The mysterious death of a former police commissioner forces a reluctant Morse to team up with old professional rival Chief Inspector Dawson. As they and Sergeant Lewis begin to investigate, a link is uncovered to the unsolved case of a girl murdered 18 years previously. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Kenneth Colley and Christopher Eccleston(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: After reaching breaking point with being followed around by Buddy, Martin resolves to find a vet to have it put down, while Ruth finds out that Bert has been trying to make his own whisky. Clive is suspicious of Mrs Tishell and who she might be cooking meals for, Penhale makes a hash of asking Janice out, and Louisa and Martin's therapy homework results in a picnic on the beach, where they are interrupted by local vet Angela Sim - who appears to be hallucinating. Comedy drama, guest starring Caroline Quentin(n)

credits.actor: Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When a spate of petty thefts culminates in a series of murders at a student hostel, Miss Lemon persuades the Belgian sleuth to investigate. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis and Paris Jefferson(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis, Paris Jefferson

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian tells the story of Tulip, a 10-week-old puppy abandoned in a graveyard close to the Battersea Old Windsor site. With hopes high that Tulip can be rehomed, Paul is devastated to learn there might be something wrong with her front legs. Plus, there's the potential for lively Labrador Scooby to be a search-and-rescue dog, but only if he can pass his exams(n)

programme.title: Tonight at the Palladium
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the variety show, featuring the cast of Beautiful: The Carole King Musical, with a medley of songs from the show, as well as former Westlife singer Markus Feehily and one-time Strictly dancers Vincent Simone and Flavia Cacace. Hans Klok, who describes himself as the world's fastest illusionist, teaches Jimmy a few tricks, there's comedy from Rob Beckett, and further music from Rebecca Ferguson and 1980s chart stars Deacon Blue(n)

programme.title: The Theory of Everything
programme.desc: Biopic of Stephen Hawking, exploring the renowned astrophysicist's romance with future wife Jane during their time at university in the 1960s and his initial diagnosis with motor neurone disease, which doctors believed would lead to his death within two years. Undaunted by deteriorating health, he continued his groundbreaking research into the origins of the universe. Starring an Oscar-winning Eddie Redmayne, with Felicity Jones and David Thewlis(n)

Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell, with Charles Dance and Maggie Steed(n)

credits.actor: Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Charles Dance, Maggie Steed

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Schoolteacher Connie Bishop keeps waking in the night, convinced she has an intruder, but most of the villagers in Badger's Drift dismiss her fears as an overactive imagination. Days later, a local councillor thought to have been obsessed with her is found murdered in her garden, and the ensuing investigation by Barnaby and Jones uncovers jealous rivals, ruthless social climbers and a cottage with a few secrets. Guest starring Paul Kaye, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

credits.actor: Paul Kaye, John Nettles, Jason Hughes

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective investigates the disappearance of a Dutch au pair, which seems connected to rivalry amid the city's academics. His search leads him to a family estate in the Oxfordshire countryside where he meets three siblings divided by personal demons and shared loss. Endeavour suspects there is a link between the disappearance and an unsolved case from years before, but as the investigation continues, conflict erupts between his fellow officers. When a group of teenagers makes a horrifying discovery, he suspects someone is manipulating events from afar(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The rural bliss of Ferne Basset is rudely shattered by the discovery of an elderly resident's drowned body. But when Barnaby and Troy descend on the village to ferret out the culprit, they get caught up in simmering backstage rivalries at a local amateur dramatic production of Amadeus. John Nettles stars, with Daniel Casey, Sarah Badel, Janine Duvitski and Bernard Hepton(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel tries to persuade Mark to take his exam. Carol is late for work again. Eric tries to explain to Michael that he loves Elizabeth(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Weatherill is involved in an air rescue to save an unconscious girl and finds herself braving a cliff abseil to reach the youngster. Medical drama, starring Amy Robbins, Robert Daws, Linda Armstrong and John Axon(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An elderly man reports an unusual break-in, during which the uninvited visitor left a mysterious statue on the kitchen table. It soon emerges that it is just the first in a series of unexplained events, as a police phone box arrives and then disappears from his garden, cement is delivered to his house for no apparent reason and a fire is started in his garage. Noakes advises Steve to keep well away as it is not technically a police matter, but the officer decides to investigate further. Rural drama, starring Trevor Ray, Georgie Glen and James Carlton(n)

credits.actor: Trevor Ray, Georgie Glen, James Carlton

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star(n)

credits.actor: Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton, Mark Jordon

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox(n)

programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Hemel Hempstead, Hertfordshire, where they meet deaf couple Asif Iqbal and wife Sabina. The green-fingered experts attempt to turn a boring lawn and crumbling patio into a Bollywood dream garden with an outdoor kitchen for Asif, Sabina and their family(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Nurse Maggie Simkiss cares for end of life patients and treats their emotional well-being as well as their direct medical needs. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: The Theory of Everything

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris travels through the Middle East to explore what remains of the colonial railways, which were built there more than 100 years ago. In the Wadi Rum valley, Chris boards a steam train along one of the few bits of the Hejaz railway that remains in working use, following the route through Jordan, before crossing the border into Israel as he heads for Jerusalem, which is sacred to more than half of the world's population. Along the way, Chris visits the ancient city of Petra, as well as Haifa and Tel Aviva(n)

programme.title: Crossrail: Where Did It All Go Wrong?
programme.desc: Crossrail is Europe's biggest engineering project, a new train line linking east and west London that was meant to open in December last year, but it is years behind schedule, billions over budget and dogged by all kinds of problems. This documentary tracks the troubled project from the very beginning, featuring interviews with former workers, politicians and transport experts, as well as people who were promised that Crossrail would bring huge benefits(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go Barging

Amanda takes a detour to Warwick Castle in search of her ancestors, while Sean and John, discover the unique joys of sheep-racing. Deep into Shakespeare country, Michael tries to find the famous local pies he remembers from his youth(n)

programme.desc: Examining the devastation caused in Carlisle when severe flooding in 2015 left most of the city underwater just before Christmas. Cameras also cover a disastrous basement conversion in a north London flat, a botched boiler installation in Manchester, and a Shropshire-based rental nightmare(n)

programme.title: D-Day's Sunken Secrets
programme.desc: Using two mini-submarines, a group of historians, wreck detectives, scientists and D-Day veterans go on an expedition to search the seabed for items from the Normandy landings of June 1944. Among the objects the team finds are a collection of American Sherman tanks that were specially adapted to float, and the wreck of the SS Leopoldville, a troopship sunk by a U-boat in December 1944 with the loss of more than 800 lives. Richard Armitage narrates(n)

programme.title: WW2: The True Cost of War
programme.sub-title: The War of the World/Lightning War
programme.desc: Documentary using recorded figures and statistics to outline the full extent of the conflict, explaining the horrors of war and how it ever came to take place. The programme begins by looking at the story of Franz Honiok, a 43-year-old farmer who is often considered the first victim of the Second World War, before going on to show that when Germany invaded Poland in August 1939, no-one was ready for war(n)

programme.title: Schindler's List
programme.desc: In German-occupied Poland during the Second World War, businessman and Nazi Party member Oskar Schindler is deeply affected when he witnesses the clearing of the Krakow Ghetto. He decides to try to save Jews destined for the concentration camps by employing them in his factory - an act of mercy made possible by a deal struck with a sadistic German commandant. Steven Spielberg's Oscar-winning drama, starring Liam Neeson, Ben Kingsley and Ralph Fiennes(n)

programme.title: Bad Tenants, Rogue Landlords
programme.desc: Catching up with previous stories to find out what happened next. The property ended up in a terrible state of repair - but worst of all, the landlords faced verbal threats and behaviour. Plus, the story of a tanning salon owner who offered an employee his home to rent, only for her to stop paying and turn it into a party house(n)

programme.title: Bad Tenants, Rogue Landlords

Plus, a young mum locked out of her home by the young couple she allowed to stay there for free while the wife had a baby, and a landlord who reduced her tenants' rent when they got into financial difficulties - only for the payments to end altogether(n)

programme.title: Bad Tenants, Rogue Landlords
programme.desc: An edition catching up with previous cases. Owed thousands in rent, landlord Matthew decided to evict his tenant, and called on an eviction specialist for help. As the tenant lived with seven cats, Matthew was worried about the state of the property, but that turned out to be the least of his problems. Cameras catch up with Matthew to see if he turned his property around. Having found a buyer for his house, Kirk asked the sitting tenant to leave. However, on the day of the move, she refused, stopped paying rent and Kirk's sale fell through. Kirk had to evict his nightmare tenant before he lost the house. Was he able to sell his property at least, or was he tempted to become a landlord once again?(n)

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: The Colemans from Salisbury swap homes, budgets and lives with the Morgan family from Hampshire, who are intrigued to know what life is like for the top 10-per-cent earners in the country. The Colemans have a large four-bedroom house, enjoy watersports and music festivals and have a weekly budget of about £1,800. The Morgans, meanwhile, who live in a second-floor flat, get by on £158 a week(n)

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Skint
programme.desc: Observational series following rich kids who move in with families struggling to make ends meet(n)

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world(n)

programme.title: Summer Body: The Big Fat Lies About Diet & Exercise
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the potential pitfalls of trying to lead a more healthy lifestyle, from the dangers of crash dieting to the unexpected side effects of doing too much exercise. The programme features contributions from nutritionists, dieticians, GPs, cardiologists, fitness trainers and psychologists, who explain the health benefits of fat and carbohydrates, the addictive quality of sugar, the hazards of diet pills, and what can happen to someone who eats too many bananas(n)

programme.title: My Crazy Rich Asian Wedding

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Richard III in Middleham, North Yorkshire, a pub run by Ken Ward and his wife Sharon, who become suspicious when one couple check out before breakfast. The village is known for its horse racing links, but one of the competitors, Matt Wilson, has a fear of horses, and another couple are concerned at staying above a noisy pub(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Court House Unique Boutique B&B in Shelsley Walsh, Worcestershire, where hosts Keith and Lisa Shearin hope to impress. On arrival, Ken and Sharon are blown away by their honeymoon suite and the seemingly low price. Suspicions over price fixing drive Matt and Paul to the internet to verify the room rate, where they discover an anomaly, and despite Keith's best efforts at breakfast, Jo and Louis dismiss it as being too fancy(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Castley Camp in Otley, West Yorkshire, for the third day of the contest, owned by single mum Jo Stevenson and her son Louis. Some of the guests are horrified by stains on their bedding and dust in their room, while an afternoon boot camp fitness session reveals Keith's competitive side. At feedback, Jo is left fuming over the comments about cleanliness and breakfast(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at the Lion & Swan in Congleton, Cheshire, run by owner Paul Whyle and manager Matt Wilson. A dirty bathroom and a waterbed cause upset, while some guests are still fuming about previous negative feedback. However, the worst is left for breakfast, when Sharon reveals she has discovered their rooms online at a higher price than they were told, upsetting Keith and Lisa and leaving the hosts determined to clear their names(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. First up are Ken and Sharon from the Richard III, who want to learn more about what's been causing Jo and Louis to lose sleep. Next, Keith and Lisa from the Court House Unique Boutique B&B want to challenge Matt about the allegations of price fixing and vehemently fight their corner. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: The property experts visit south London, as Phil Spencer helps childhood sweethearts Cleo and Ben find a home together in Brixton on a budget of £500,000. Meanwhile, Kirstie Allsopp meets first-time buyer Charli, who wants to get her foot on the property ladder in and around Croydon, with £280,000 to spend(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the hidden lives of the animals of Chester Zoo, one of Britain's most popular zoos, using micro-rig technology to capture the behaviour of the animals and their close relationships with their keepers. In the first episode, the zoo's Asian elephant family welcome a new arrival. Grumpy matriarch Thi has been pregnant for almost two years and is reaching the end of her tether. She's given birth to a stillborn baby in the past, so the keepers have a close eye on her. Cameras also follow Red panda Jung, who has been getting the cold shoulder from his partner Nima since they had two cubs(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A two-year-old Bengal cat is rushed in after being hit by a car, and when it turns out to have a broken back and a dislocated spine, Noel Fitzpatrick agrees to do what he can - but he makes no promises. A German shepherd requires a total elbow replacement, a difficult operation using a custom printed implant system, and a rottweiler puppy is treated for a genetic condition that has destroyed the cartilage on its ankle bone(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
